tracking.controllers: Index / Vive Wand (use any Lighthouse-tracked controllers)

knownIssues.title: DisplayPort Display Stream Compression (DSC) is mandatory
knownIssues.description: At 2880×2720 per eye @ 90 Hz, the Aero requires DSC to fit through a single DisplayPort 1.4 cable. If your GPU or cable doesn't properly support DSC, the headset falls back to a lower refresh rate or shows color banding.
knownIssues.workaround: Use the Varjo-supplied cable. Verify DSC is active in Varjo Base → Headset → Advanced. NVIDIA 20-series and newer support DSC; older cards may struggle.

knownIssues.title: Dynamic foveated rendering requires eye calibration
knownIssues.description: The Aero's main selling point — dynamic foveated rendering (rendering full detail only where your eyes look) — requires completing eye calibration in Varjo Base. If skipped, you get the full perf cost of 2880×2720 per eye.
knownIssues.workaround: Varjo Base → Eyes → Calibrate. Repeat calibration if you wear different glasses or other users share the headset.

knownIssues.title: SteamVR base stations required — no inside-out tracking
knownIssues.description: The Aero has no cameras for inside-out tracking. You need at least one (ideally two) Valve Index or HTC Vive base stations mounted in your play space.
knownIssues.workaround: Lighthouse 2.0 base stations are recommended. Mount at opposite corners 2m+ above the floor angled downward.

optimizationTips.tip: Enable Dynamic Foveated Rendering for a 30-40% GPU headroom increase
optimizationTips.simpleExplanation: The Aero can render only what your eyes are looking at in full detail. Turn on foveated rendering in Varjo Base to reclaim significant GPU headroom.
optimizationTips.advancedExplanation: Dynamic FR reduces effective pixel shading by ~40% with near-zero perceptual cost when eye-tracking is calibrated. It's one of the largest single performance gains available on this platform. Varjo Base → Display → Foveated Rendering → On.

optimizationTips.tip: Use OpenXR Toolkit for additional upscaling
optimizationTips.simpleExplanation: OpenXR Toolkit (free) layers over Varjo Base and adds NIS/FSR/CAS upscaling options that work on top of foveated rendering.
optimizationTips.advancedExplanation: Install OpenXR Toolkit → configure via the in-VR overlay (Ctrl+F2). FSR Performance mode + foveated rendering stacks multiplicatively and can yield 60%+ GPU savings.

optimizationTips.tip: Set render resolution around 80% in SteamVR — the Aero's native res is already extremely high
optimizationTips.simpleExplanation: The Aero's per-eye resolution is already high enough that SteamVR's default supersampling is overkill on most GPUs.
optimizationTips.advancedExplanation: Target ~80% render resolution in SteamVR (≈2304×2176 per eye). This leaves headroom for in-app anti-aliasing which is often more impactful than raw resolution at this DPI.
